rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES FREDERICK DAUGHERTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/9/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The wrong form of notice was used. Form DE-120 was updated in 2020. (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) The first Exhibit A was not attached to the petition (the letter from the FTB). (4) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (5) It is noted that 180 days from the date the administrator signed this pleading requesting an extension of time would mean 5/14/23 Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/28/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SAMUEL V. CHONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Heirship (Probate Code Section 11700) And For Order To Increase Bond
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/25/2023. The following procedural issues must be cleared two weeks prior to the next hearing. The issues have not been cleared since the tentative ruling posted for 1/10/2023 hearing. 1) Notice to Mae, Lotus and Christine. If each person's whereabouts have been unknown to petitioner, a declaration of diligent search pursuant to CRC 7.52 is required. Listing the names on the notice filed on 12/20/22 without addresses appears insufficient. 2) Petition to set forth the names and the addresses of the heirs who would file the affidavits under PC 13100. 3) Petition states that there would be no probate proceeding in CA or Philippines; thus, notice of hearing to "estate of Lorna J. Gonazales" or "Estate of Peter Kekanuna" should be given to all intestate heirs unless a court have ordered otherwise.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MELVIN FLEMING
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/3/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. Still no proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] 2. Now an amended petition has been filed. No courtesy copy was received by the examiner by the 3/3/23 deadline or even as of 3/9/23. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B] 3. The amended petition was not e-filed until 3/7/23, when it was first available for viewing on the Court's website and which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. 4. No proof of service has been filed in any event. The proposed order and courtesy copies of the amended petition and any other papers filed thereafter must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SONIA GUTIERREZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/9/23. (No earlier available date.) (1) Still no proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] A cursory review of the petition online indicates that the decedent died on 8/29/14. The initial petition claimed that the date of death was 1/14/21. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) The assignment filed on 7/20/22 was not signed by Juan Gutierrez and the jurat pertains to just Sila Alvarenga's signature. (5) The administrator is not the sole distributee. The first page of the assignment purportedly executed by Juan Gutierrez shows that only the interest in the real property was assigned. Deadline is 4/28/23 for delivering courtesy copies to the assigned examiner of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues and new proof of service. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEXANDER WITHERILL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Exhibit D (changes in the form of assets) was not very helpful; "change in investment value resulting in decrease" is not meaningful. Changes in investment values do not increase or decrease the number of shares of securities; changes in the number of shares due to sales or other reinvested dividends result in decreases or increases to values. Paragraph 23 and 24b of the petition do not appear to apply to this estate as there was no real property in the estate. Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed. Only shares of securities, not their current (constantly fluctuating) value, should be distributed. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT KENTON KISTLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/9/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) It is unclear why this petition is necessary as to the two IRAs if it is the default procedure of the financial institutions to distribute to a surviving spouse. The personal representative can simply correct her I&A to remove assets that are later discovered not to be estate assets. (The I&A does not refer to any of the assets being part of a "Mirantis" account ending in 9140.) (2) As to the Bank of America account, it appears that either a Spousal Property petition or even a petition under Probate Code section 850 might be more appropriate if the bank will not release the funds. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/28/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
